name: Detailed Scope of Work for Office Renovation

chat_messages.text: Please pull the detail scope of work section from this pdf and list it out on here

attachments.extracted_content:
September 28, 2024
Proposal No.: 2024-62
Job Site: 1200 sq ft Office Space Renovation
Project No. 2409-05
Attention: [Landlord's Name]
Observations: Site visit and initial scope of work exploration conducted on 09/27/2024. Key
findings include:
Unit description: Site visit and initial scope of work exploration conducted on 09/27/2024. The
unit is approximately 1200 sqft (65ft by 20ft) with one rear entrance/exit located in the back of
the build on the second floor. There are two flights of stairs leading to the front door. The Entry
door is through an oversized metal security door, currently secured by a padlock. The interior
comprises of two offices: Room 1 (10.5'x10.5'x9't, south) and Room 2 (10.5'x10'x5'x9't, north),
each featuring a 4.5'x8' divided lite metal frame window and operable wood entry door. Behind
Room 2 are two closet spaces (approx. 5'w x 15'l x 9't). A central main area (approx. 35'x19'x9't)
includes two metal frame divided light windows located in the middle of the two rooms. THe
interior walls are metal-framed painted drywall with no apparent electrical in the walls. The
ceiling consists of original wood slats with plaster and some drywall layers, while the east wall is
a combination of wood slats, plaster, drywall, and paint. The west wall is brick, and the roof uses
a redwood flat truss system. Flooring is newly installed low-quality vinyl over an uneven
substructure, with underlying plywood and structural framing issues to be addressed. The unit
includes a bathroom (toilet, shower, sink in poor condition), an installed fire sprinkler system, a
sub-panel in the north closet area, and a disconnected hot water unit in the bathroom.
NOTES:
- 2 utility style sinks with running cold water and working drains, poor condition
- washer and dryer hookups with clogged drain (foam present) outlet and breaker amp TBD.
The hot and cold water lines need to be tested as well as a drain maintenance and test.
- Bathroom: Fiberglass shower with non operable water.
-1 toilet in poor condition. water flushes.
- doors, located 6 wood interior doors. All doors are operable, hinges and door handles present
- windows: in fair condition, cracked windows, missing window panes, some window mechanics
need fixing
- Ceiling: Drop ceiling partially demoed
- Flooring: Relatively new but non-leveled, missing base moldings
- Walls: Brick walls painted multiple colors, existing painted drywall in fair condition
- Kitchen: No existing kitchen or counter space
- Other features: Past existing skylight, no fire escape, installed fire alarms and sprinklers
- Issues: Small amount of mold on interior drywall (possible small roof leak)
-signs of vermin rodents and insects. Pigeon excrements and roach carcass’s present
Job Site: 1200 sq ft Office Space – Project No. 2409-05
1

September 28, 2024
Proposal No.: 2024-62
Project Scope: To renovate and modernize unit [insert number] at [insert address], an
approximately 1200 square foot office space, over 6-8 months. Corey Smith will manage the
work, overseen by Nick . The design features a semi-open floor plan combining permanent
walls with interchangeable architectural panels. These versatile panels, made from low-cost,
lightweight materials, can be moved to expanded, contracted, or removed as needed. Exposed
wood truss ceiling and brick walls will be painted white to enhance spaciousness. Custom-built,
industrial-themed kitchen and bathroom will complete the transformation. APP controlled RGB
led lighting will be installed in the exposed wood rafters. The result: a flexible, modern space
adaptable to the changing needs and demands of business. .
Detailed scope of work
I. Pre-Construction:
1. CREATE BUDGET CONSCIOUS INTERIOR DESIGN PLAN
2. MATERIAL ANALYSIS AND SELECTION
3. BUDGET FORMULATION & FINALIZATION
4. CREATE A CONSTRUCTION TIMELINE
II. Demolition and Preparation:
1. DEMO PRE-EXISTING INTERIOR DRYWALL WALLS AND DOORS
2. REMOVE DROP CEILINGS IN FRONT MAIN AREA AND BACK ROOMS
3. REMOVE PRE-EXISTING FLOORING IN ENTIRE SPACE
4. PREP FOR SELF-LEVELING
a. REMOVE BROKEN OR COMPROMISED SHEETING AS NEEDED
b. REPLACE, SECURE, FASTEN, STRENGTHEN ANY COMPROMISED
FLOOR FRAMING
c. FRAME AND LEVEL AS NEEDED
5. REMOVE OLD ELECTRICAL AND PLUMBING RUNS IN CEILING AS NEEDED
6. PREPARE EXPOSED CEILING MEMBERS, BRICK WALLS FOR PAINTING
III. Plumbing and Electrical:
1. INSTALL ON-DEMAND WATER HEATER AND PLUMBING SYSTEM
2. VALIDATE EXISTING PLUMBING AND FIX ANY PRE-EXISTING PLUMBING
ISSUES
3. MOVE WATER AND DRAIN LINES AS NEEDED
4. VALIDATE CURRENT ELECTRICAL SYSTEM
5. INSTALL METER TO SUB-PANEL (BY OWNER)
6. REROUTE SUB PANEL MAIN POWER RUN IN CEILING
Job Site: 1200 sq ft Office Space – Project No. 2409-05
2

September 28, 2024
Proposal No.: 2024-62
7. RE-ROUTE ANY ELECTRICAL LINES FROM SUBPANEL IN CEILING AS
NEEDED (BY OWNER)
8. DESIGN AND IMPLEMENT NEW ELECTRICAL SCHEMATIC
9. INSTALL NEW POWER RUNS FROM THE BREAKER BOX TO THE
ELECTRICAL OUTLETS, SWITCHES AND JUNCTIONS AS NEEDED PER
DESIGN (BY OWNER)
10. INSTALL HOUSE LIGHTING IN CEILING THROUGHOUT PER ELECTRICAL
PLAN
11. INSTALL FIRE EXTINGUISHERS AND ILLUMINATED EXIT SIGNS FOR FIRE
SAFETY
12. MOVE HARD WIRED CARBON DIOXIDE/ SMOKE DETECTORS AS NEEDED.
IV. Bathroom Demo and Build-Out:
1. REMOVE - EXISTING TILE and vinyl FLOORing
2. REPLACE/REPAIR ANY COMPROMISED SUBFLOOR SHEATHING AND OR
STRUCTURAL FRAMING MEMBERS.
3. REPAIR, REPLACE DRYWALL AS NEEDED
4. LEVEL FLOORS AND PLUMB WALLS AS NEEDED.
5. REMOVE EXISTING TOILET AND DISPOSE
6. REMOVE EXISTING SINK AND DISPOSE
7. REMOVE EXISTING SHOWER SHOWER AND DISPOSE
8. MOVE THE WEST BATHROOM WALL TWO FEET.
9. BUILD FRAMEWORK FOR SHOWER FLOOR AND CURB
10. PRE-PLUM SHOWER DRAIN CONNECTORS
11. RUN HOT AND COLD SHOWER LINES TO SHOWER MIXING VALVE
12. INSTALL SHOWER MIXING VALVE
13. INSTALL WATER LINE FOR SHOWER HEAD.
14. INSTALL FELT MOISTURE BARRIER IN SHOWER AREA
15. INSTALL 1/2 ‘ GREEN BOARD TO SHOWER WALLS. SILICONE SEAL
AROUND THE EDGES.
16. GLUE AND CONNECT SHOWER DRAIN FLANGE TO THE SHOWER DRAIN
LINE.. SECURE FLANGE TO SUBFLOOR.
17. POUR DECK MUD PRE-SLOPE TO THE DRAIN FLANGE LEVEL. POUR DECK
MUD TO PREMARKED SLOPE LINES. SILICONE SEAL EDGES.
18. INSTALL P.V.C. WATERPROOF MEMBRANE OVER PRE-SLOPE
19. INSTALL HARDIEBACKER TO SHOWER WALLS
20. TAPE AND SEAL ANY GAPS AND SCREW HOLES. SILICONE SEAL BOTTOM
GAPS AS NEEDED.
21. POUR FINAL DECK MUD SLOP. ADD METAL GRID REINFORCEMENTS.
22. INSTALL TILE TYPE 1 IN BATHROOM SHOWER AREA.
23. INSTALL TILE TYPE 2 IN THE MAIN BATHROOM AREA.
Job Site: 1200 sq ft Office Space – Project No. 2409-05
3

September 28, 2024
Proposal No.: 2024-62
24. FABRICATE AND INSTALL METAL FRAMED GLASS SHOWER ENCLOSURE.
WITH ONE FIXED PANEL AND ONE SWINGING GLASS DOOR WITH FRAME.
25. INSTALL TOILET WATER LINES AND ANGLE STOP VALVE
26. INSTALL NEW TOILET AND WAX RING. CONNECT PLUMBING
27. BUILD AND INSTALL CUSTOM METAL VANITY CABINET WITH AN IN WALL
RECESSED MIRROR WITH DIFFUSED SIDE LIGHTING
28. BUILD CAST FRAMING AND POUR CEMENT COUNTER TOP
29. SAND AND POLISH FINISH. T.B.D.
30. SEAL COUNTERTOP WITH FOOD AND MEDICAL GRADE CLEAR
31. RUN ELECTRICAL FOR THE FAN, LIGHTS, OUTLETS AND SWITCHES.
32. INSTALL BATHROOM FAN
33. INSTALL BATHROOM LIGHT
34. RUN HOT AND COLD WATER LINES FOR THE SINK
35. INSTALL STAINLESS STEEL SINK TO CEMENT COUNTER TOP.
36. INSTALL P TRAP AND PLUMBING COMPONENTS.
37. INSTALL FAUCET AND WATER LINES
38. DRYWALL, TAPING AND MUDDING AS NEEDED.
39. PRIME AND PAINT WALLS FINISH PAINT WILL BE SEMI GLOSS
40. INSTALL BASEBOARDS
V. Kitchen Build-Out:
1. FABRICATE AND INSTALL METAL AND WOOD CABINETS per approved
design
2. FABRICATE CUSTOM CEMENT COUNTER TOP
3. MIX AND POUR CEMENT WITH EPOXY ADDITIVE AND COLORED DYE
4. SAND AND POLISH COUNTERTOP, CLEAN UP EDGES
5. APPLY FOOD GRADE CLEAR COATING
6. INSTALL STAINLESS FARMHOUSE STYLE SINK AND DRAIN
7. INSTALL GARBAGE DISPOSER
8. INSTALL SINK AND PLUMBING
9. INSTALL FAUCET WATER LINES
10. INSTALL LED KITCHEN LIGHT
11. INSTALL 2 GFI 4 OUTPUT POWER OUTLETS ABOVE COUNTER
12. INSTALL GARBAGE DISPOSAL AND DISHWASHER WASHER UNIT
13. INSTALL POWER OUTLET FOR THE REFRIGERATOR/STOVE
14. INSTALL REFRIGERATOR
15. INSTALL POWER FOR STOVE AS NEEDED
16. INSTALL STOVE/OVEN
17. INSTALL MICROWAVE.
Job Site: 1200 sq ft Office Space – Project No. 2409-05
4

September 28, 2024
Proposal No.: 2024-62
VI. Main Room Build-Out:
1. INSTALL DRYWALL ON THE EAST WALL
2. PATCH AND PAINT DRYWALL ON EAST WALL
3. FRAME AND ADD DRYWALL TO INTERIOR WALLS FOR ROOMS 1 AND 2
PER DESIGN
4. PATCH AND PAINT DRY WALL FOR ROOMS 1 AND 2 PER DESIGN
5. PREP AND PAINT CEILING, WALLS, AND EXPOSED BRICK
6. ADD SELF-LEVELING AS NEEDED THROUGHOUT SPACE
7. FINAL PAINTING AND TOUCH-UPS
8. FABRICATE EIGHT 4’X8’ ARCHITECTURAL MOVABLE WALLS AND BARN
DOOR. FOR ROOMS 1 AND 2
9. FABRICATE AND INSTALL MOVABLE WALL BRACKET SYSTEM
10. INSTALL FLOORING
11. INSTALL BASE MOLDING AND TRIM AS NEEDED
12. BUILD OUT UTILITY CLOSET AREA ON THE EAST WALL FOR WASHER
DRYER AND STORAGE.
13. BUILD UTLITY COUNTER. AND SHELVING
14. INSTALL CLOSET LIGHT.
VII. Windows and Doors
1. REPLACE ALL WINDOW GLASS PANES WITH NEW RATED GLASS PANES.
2. PREP & PAINT METAL WINDOW FRAMES
3. FIX ANY MECHANICAL ISSUES
4. REPAIR/ ADD NEW SECURITY LOCK TO FRONT METAL SECURITY DOOR
5. ADD 3 SKYLIGHTS AND 1 FOLD OPEN SKYLIGHT (FOR FIRE SAFETY EXIT)
6. INSTALL BARN DOORS & TRACK SYSTEMS TO ROOM 1 & 2
7. INSTALL NEW SWINGING DOOR AND FRAME FOR BATHROOM/KITCHEN
DOOR ACCESS
8. INSTALL NEW SWINGING DOOR AND FRAME FROM ROOM 2 TO
BATHROOM
9. IN ROOM 2: INSTALL CLOSET SECURITY DOOR.
10. IN ROOM 1: INSTALL DOOR AND FRAME ON THE EAST WALL.
11. INSTALL DOOR TRIMS TO ALL DOORS AND OPENINGS
Key Features:
❖ 
Industrial modern tech theme throughout
Job Site: 1200 sq ft Office Space – Project No. 2409-05
5

September 28, 2024
Proposal No.: 2024-62
❖ 
Open floor plan with evolving wall panels
❖ 
Custom-built industrial cabinets materials and finsihes.
❖ 
Leveled floors with high traffic engineered flooring
❖ 
Smart home/office tech
❖ 
Skylighting through out the open space.
❖ 
Room for future expansion and versatility
Assumptions and Exclusions:
❖ 
All work will be performed by us under this scope of work. Anything not mentioned above
is not included in the scope and may affect budget and timeline. A case by case
assessment will be made regarding any deviations from the approved scope of work (the
tenants) unless otherwise noted (e.g., sub-panel installation).
❖ 
Landlord to provide, procure furnish ANY BUILDING MATERIALS OR ASSOCIATED
CONSTRUCTION COST. .
❖ 
The landlord will provide necessary permissions and access for the duration of the
renovation. Any required permits or inspections will be obtained and scheduled by the
landlord.
❖ 
Major structural changes are not included in this proposal.
❖ 
Unforeseen issues (e.g., mold, asbestos) are not included in this scope and will be
addressed separately if discovered.
❖ 
All debris removal and disposal is included in our scope of work. Debris container and
disposal fees paid for by the owner.
❖ 
We will maintain a clean and safe work environment throughout the renovation process.
❖ 
All construction materials that can be reclaimed and reused for the renovations will be
saved for re-use.
❖ 
All approved electrical work per plans will be provided by others.
Project Timeline:
The renovation will be completed in 5 phases over an estimated 6-8 month period:
Phase 1: Interior Design Layout (2 weeks)
Phase 2: Front Room and Main Area Demo (1 month)
Phase 2: Back room Demo and Systems Update (1 month)
Phase 4: Kitchen and Bath DEMO and Build-Out (3 months)
Phase 5: Construction and Finishing (2 months)
TERMS and conditions
❖ 
Budget Tracking: A detailed line-item budget sheet will be maintained for this project.
The owner and managing agents shall have unrestricted access to this sheet at all times.
Job Site: 1200 sq ft Office Space – Project No. 2409-05
6

September 28, 2024
Proposal No.: 2024-62
❖ 
Material Purchases: Only materials explicitly approved in the Scope of Work (SOW) shall
be procured for this project.
❖ 
Weekly Budget Allocation: A weekly materials budget, amount to be determined, will be
allocated based on estimated material costs per phase and/or month.
❖ 
Labor Tracking: Labor hours will be allocated to each phase of the build, tracked, and
updated on a weekly basis.
❖ 
Material Costs: It is agreed that all materials and associated costs for this project shall
be provided by the owner.
❖ 
Contractor Responsibilities: The contractor (hereinafter referred to as "we" or "our") shall
be responsible for providing the following services: a) Project management b) Interior
design c) Hard construction labor, including but not limited to:
➢ 
Framing
➢ 
Drywall installation
➢ 
Welding
➢ 
Plumbing
➢ 
Pre-tile work (including framing, sloping, water sealing, hardiebacker installation,
and finish taping)
➢ 
Painting
➢ 
Lighting fixture and LED installation
➢ 
Cabinetry fabrication and installation
➢ 
Countertop fabrication and installation
➢ 
Finish carpentry
We, Corey Smith and Zayne Amason, guarantee that the above work will be performed in
accordance with the outlined scope and timeline. We propose to furnish the project
management and labor for the scope of work above in a timely and professional manner. Al
work to be performed according to industry construction standards. Our services will be
provided in a substantial, workmanlike manner in exchange for a set number of monthly rental
credits. The monthly fair market rate of the rental property will not exceed the monthly labor
allotment. If the value of labor performed exceeds the rental rate, a credit will be added to our
tenant ledger. If we fail to provide the services mentioned above in this scope of work penalties
(TBD) and our contract termination may be enforced. The exact terms of this projects credit
system are to be determined. This arrangement ensures a fair exchange of our services for the
agreed-upon rental credits, with provisions for additional compensation should our work exceed
the predetermined monthly value. If work is done sooner than the propose finish date (TBD)
lease rental credits will apply from tenant ledger towards future rents.
(Lease and contract need to be finalized and discuss further)
Approved and accepted by:
__________________________________________
Job Site: 1200 sq ft Office Space – Project No. 2409-05
7

September 28, 2024
Proposal No.: 2024-62
Signature
__________________________________________
Print Name
__________________________________________
Title
__________________________________________
Date

chat_messages.text:
Certainly. Here's the detailed scope of work extracted from the document:




# Detailed Scope of Work

## I. Pre-Construction:
1. Create budget conscious interior design plan
2. Material analysis and selection
3. Budget formulation & finalization
4. Create a construction timeline

## II. Demolition and Preparation:
1. Demo pre-existing interior drywall walls and doors
2. Remove drop ceilings in front main area and back rooms
3. Remove pre-existing flooring in entire space
4. Prep for self-leveling
   a. Remove broken or compromised sheeting as needed
   b. Replace, secure, fasten, strengthen any compromised floor framing
   c. Frame and level as needed
5. Remove old electrical and plumbing runs in ceiling as needed
6. Prepare exposed ceiling members, brick walls for painting

## III. Plumbing and Electrical:
1. Install on-demand water heater and plumbing system
2. Validate existing plumbing and fix any pre-existing plumbing issues
3. Move water and drain lines as needed
4. Validate current electrical system
5. Install meter to sub-panel (by owner)
6. Reroute sub panel main power run in ceiling
7. Re-route any electrical lines from subpanel in ceiling as needed (by owner)
8. Design and implement new electrical schematic
9. Install new power runs from the breaker box to the electrical outlets, switches and junctions as needed per design (by owner)
10. Install house lighting in ceiling throughout per electrical plan
11. Install fire extinguishers and illuminated exit signs for fire safety
12. Move hard wired carbon dioxide/ smoke detectors as needed

## IV. Bathroom Demo and Build-Out:
1. Remove existing tile and vinyl flooring
2. Replace/repair any compromised subfloor sheathing and or structural framing members
3. Repair, replace drywall as needed
4. Level floors and plumb walls as needed
5. Remove existing toilet and dispose
6. Remove existing sink and dispose
7. Remove existing shower and dispose
8. Move the west bathroom wall two feet
9. Build framework for shower floor and curb
10. Pre-plum shower drain connectors
11. Run hot and cold shower lines to shower mixing valve
12. Install shower mixing valve
13. Install water line for shower head
14. Install felt moisture barrier in shower area
15. Install 1/2" green board to shower walls. Silicone seal around the edges
16. Glue and connect shower drain flange to the shower drain line. Secure flange to subfloor
17. Pour deck mud pre-slope to the drain flange level. Pour deck mud to premarked slope lines. Silicone seal edges
18. Install P.V.C. waterproof membrane over pre-slope
19. Install Hardiebacker to shower walls
20. Tape and seal any gaps and screw holes. Silicone seal bottom gaps as needed
21. Pour final deck mud slope. Add metal grid reinforcements
22. Install tile type 1 in bathroom shower area
23. Install tile type 2 in the main bathroom area
24. Fabricate and install metal framed glass shower enclosure with one fixed panel and one swinging glass door with frame
25. Install toilet water lines and angle stop valve
26. Install new toilet and wax ring. Connect plumbing
27. Build and install custom metal vanity cabinet with an in-wall recessed mirror with diffused side lighting
28. Build cast framing and pour cement counter top
29. Sand and polish finish. T.B.D.
30. Seal countertop with food and medical grade clear
31. Run electrical for the fan, lights, outlets and switches
32. Install bathroom fan
33. Install bathroom light
34. Run hot and cold water lines for the sink
35. Install stainless steel sink to cement counter top
36. Install P trap and plumbing components
37. Install faucet and water lines
38. Drywall, taping and mudding as needed
39. Prime and paint walls. Finish paint will be semi gloss
40. Install baseboards

## V. Kitchen Build-Out:
1. Fabricate and install metal and wood cabinets per approved design
2. Fabricate custom cement counter top
3. Mix and pour cement with epoxy additive and colored dye
4. Sand and polish countertop, clean up edges
5. Apply food grade clear coating
6. Install stainless farmhouse style sink and drain
7. Install garbage disposer
8. Install sink and plumbing
9. Install faucet water lines
10. Install LED kitchen light
11. Install 2 GFI 4 output power outlets above counter
12. Install garbage disposal and dishwasher washer unit
13. Install power outlet for the refrigerator/stove
14. Install refrigerator
15. Install power for stove as needed
16. Install stove/oven
17. Install microwave

## VI. Main Room Build-Out:
1. Install drywall on the east wall
2. Patch and paint drywall on east wall
3. Frame and add drywall to interior walls for rooms 1 and 2 per design
4. Patch and paint drywall for rooms 1 and 2 per design
5. Prep and paint ceiling, walls, and exposed brick
6. Add self-leveling as needed throughout space
7. Final painting and touch-ups
8. Fabricate eight 4'x8' architectural movable walls and barn door for rooms 1 and 2
9. Fabricate and install movable wall bracket system
10. Install flooring
11. Install base molding and trim as needed
12. Build out utility closet area on the east wall for washer dryer and storage
13. Build utility counter and shelving
14. Install closet light

## VII. Windows and Doors:
1. Replace all window glass panes with new rated glass panes
2. Prep & paint metal window frames
3. Fix any mechanical issues
4. Repair/ add new security lock to front metal security door
5. Add 3 skylights and 1 fold open skylight (for fire safety exit)
6. Install barn doors & track systems to room 1 & 2
7. Install new swinging door and frame for bathroom/kitchen door access
8. Install new swinging door and frame from room 2 to bathroom
9. In room 2: Install closet security door
10. In room 1: Install door and frame on the east wall
11. Install door trims to all doors and openings


This detailed scope of work outlines the renovation plan for the 1200 sq ft office space. It covers all aspects of the project, from pre-construction planning to the final touches on windows and doors. The scope is divided into seven main sections, each focusing on a specific area or type of work to be done.

Is there any specific part of the scope you'd like me to elaborate on or explain further?
for each section except pre-construction can you create a budget, do not factor in labor cost, or tools, just focus on building materials

Certainly! I'll create a budget estimate for the building materials for each section of the renovation project, excluding labor costs and tools. Please note that these are rough estimates based on average prices, and actual costs may vary depending on the specific materials chosen, market conditions, and location.




# Estimated Building Materials Budget

## II. Demolition and Preparation:
- Dumpster rental for debris removal: $500
- Plywood for subfloor repair: $300
- Self-leveling compound: $500
- Primer for walls and ceiling: $200
**Subtotal: $1,500**

## III. Plumbing and Electrical:
- On-demand water heater: $1,000
- PEX piping and fittings: $500
- Electrical wire and conduit: $800
- Outlets, switches, and junction boxes: $300
- Light fixtures: $600
- Fire extinguishers: $200
- Exit signs: $150
- Smoke/CO detectors: $100
**Subtotal: $3,650**

## IV. Bathroom Demo and Build-Out:
- Shower system (including valve and head): $400
- Tile for shower and floor: $600
- Hardiebacker board: $100
- Waterproofing membrane: $150
- Cement board and mortar: $200
- Glass shower enclosure: $1,000
- Toilet: $250
- Vanity and sink: $500
- Faucet: $150
- Mirror: $200
- Paint: $100
- Baseboards: $100
- Bathroom fan: $100
- Miscellaneous plumbing fittings: $200
**Subtotal: $4,050**

## V. Kitchen Build-Out:
- Cabinet materials (assuming custom-built): $2,000
- Countertop materials (cement and epoxy): $500
- Sink: $300
- Faucet: $200
- Garbage disposal: $100
- Dishwasher: $500
- Refrigerator: $1,000
- Stove/oven: $800
- Microwave: $200
- Lighting fixtures: $200
**Subtotal: $5,800**

## VI. Main Room Build-Out:
- Drywall sheets: $500
- Joint compound and tape: $100
- Paint for walls and ceiling: $400
- Flooring materials: $2,400 (assuming $2/sq ft for 1200 sq ft)
- Baseboards and trim: $300
- Materials for movable walls (8 panels): $1,600
- Barn door hardware: $200
- Utility closet shelving and counter materials: $300
**Subtotal: $5,800**

## VII. Windows and Doors:
- Window glass panes: $1,000
- Paint for window frames: $100
- Security lock for front door: $100
- Skylights (3 regular, 1 fold-open): $2,000
- Barn door materials and track systems: $600
- Interior doors (3): $450
- Door frames and trim: $300
**Subtotal: $4,550**

## Total Estimated Materials Budget: $25,350

Please note:
1. These estimates are based on mid-range materials and may vary depending on the quality and brands chosen.
2. Prices can fluctuate based on market conditions and location.
3. This budget does not include any contingency for unexpected issues or changes in the scope of work.
4. Some specialized items (e.g., custom-built cabinets, movable wall systems) may have a wide range of potential costs depending on the specific design and materials chosen.
5. It's advisable to get quotes from local suppliers for more accurate pricing, especially for large-ticket items.


This budget estimate focuses solely on the building materials needed for each section of the renovation project, excluding labor costs and tools. The total estimated materials budget comes to $25,350.

Some key points to consider:

1. The largest material costs are in the Kitchen Build-Out ($5,800) and Main Room Build-Out ($5,800) sections, primarily due to appliances and flooring respectively.

2. The Bathroom Demo and Build-Out ($4,050) and Windows and Doors ($4,550) sections also represent significant material costs.

3. The Plumbing and Electrical section ($3,650) includes some major items like the on-demand water heater and electrical components.

4. The Demolition and Preparation section has the lowest material cost ($1,500), as it mainly involves removal and preparation work.

Remember that these are rough estimates and actual costs may vary. It would be wise to get specific quotes from local suppliers for more accurate pricing, especially for the more expensive items.

Would you like me to provide more detail on any specific section or item in this budget?

The total estimated materials budget for the renovation project is $25,350.

This total is derived from summing up the subtotals of each section:

1. Demolition and Preparation: $1,500
2. Plumbing and Electrical: $3,650
3. Bathroom Demo and Build-Out: $4,050
4. Kitchen Build-Out: $5,800
5. Main Room Build-Out: $5,800
6. Windows and Doors: $4,550

Total: $25,350

This budget estimate covers only the building materials for the renovation project. It does not include labor costs, tools, or any contingencies for unexpected issues. It's important to note that this is a rough estimate and actual costs may vary depending on specific material choices, market conditions, and your location.
